title: ruppia rostellata var. brachypus
intro: Ruppia rostellata var. brachypus, also known as dwarf sea lavender or dwarf ruppia, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Ruppiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. Its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable herb in traditional remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and creams for topical application.
